intent_original: Set up a static route on router r1 for the 10.3.0.0/24 prefix using r0 as the next hop.

classificacao.name: static_route_add
classificacao.rationale: The intent is to configure a static route on router r1 for the 10.3.0.0/24 prefix using r0 as the next hop.

intent_original: On r1, add a static routing entry so that traffic for subnet 10.3.0.0/24 is forwarded through r0.
Configure router r2 with a static route to reach the 10.4.0.0/24 network via r0.
Create a static route in r2 pointing the 10.4.0.0/24 subnet to gateway r0.
Provision a static route on r3 so packets destined to 10.2.0.0/24 go through router r0.
Define a static routing rule on router r3 for prefix 10.2.0.0/24 using r0.

classificacao.name: static_route_add
classificacao.rationale: This intent is to add a static routing rule on router r3 for prefix 10.2.0.0/24 using interface r0.

intent_original: Remove the static route to the 10.3.0.0/24 network from router r1.

classificacao.name: static_route_del
classificacao.rationale: The intent is to remove a static route that directs traffic to the 10.3.0.0/24 network from router r1.

intent_original: On r1, delete any static routing entry associated with subnet 10.3.0.0/24.
Ensure router r2 no longer has a route configured for the 10.4.0.0/24 prefix.

classificacao.name: static_route_del
classificacao.rationale: The intent is to remove a static route from router r2 that corresponds to the 10.4.0.0/24 prefix.

intent_original: Clear the static routing rule on r2 that forwards traffic to 10.4.0.0/24.

classificacao.name: static_route_del
classificacao.rationale: The user wants to remove a static route that forwards traffic to the specified network (10.4.0.0/24).

intent_original: Enable IPv4 packet forwarding on router r1.
Turn on IP forwarding functionality on r1.
Activate packet forwarding between interfaces on router r2.
Ensure that router r2 is configured to forward IP packets.
Set the net.ipv4.ip_forward parameter to 1 on router r3.

classificacao.name: ip_forward_enable
classificacao.rationale: This intent sets a system-wide configuration option, enabling packet forwarding at the OS/kernel level.

intent_original: Switch on IP forwarding support at the kernel level on r3.
From router r1, send ICMP echo requests to the address 10.3.0.1.
Test connectivity from r1 by pinging host 10.3.0.1.
Verify reachability from router r2 to IP 10.4.0.1 using ICMP.
On r2, run a ping test towards the host located at 10.4.0.1.
Initiate an ICMP echo test from router r3 to 10.2.0.1.
Check network connectivity by pinging 10.2.0.1 from r3.
Block ICMP packets originating from the 10.1.0.0/24 subnet on router r1.
On r1, install a rule to drop ICMP traffic sourced from 10.1.0.0/24.
Prevent ICMP echo requests from the 10.2.0.0/24 network from passing through router r2.

classificacao.name: firewall_drop_icmp_src
classificacao.rationale: This intent prevents ICMP echo requests from the 10.2.0.0/24 network from passing through router r2, which is a security measure to block malicious traffic.

Drop ICMP packets originating in the 10.3.0.0/24 subnet on router r3.
Set up a firewall rule on r3 to filter out ICMP traffic from 10.3.0.0/24.

classificacao.rationale: This intent is about blocking ICMP traffic from a specific source IP address (10.3.0.0/24) on interface r3, which is likely the router's default gateway.
